meaning: InstrE5mentElIIzEm/n.[哲]工具謘t/n.乐器演奏家,饕
examples: This special feature of language presents the embedded nature of non-instrumentalism of language.
So the lesson that Newton grew in the case of gravity and Berkeley generalized as we saw in his instrumentalism.
For a start, he does not persuade readers that realism is right and instrumentalism wrong.
He makes the mistake of attacking instrumentalism in one of its weaker versions by limiting it to the view that scientific theories are solely predictive devices.
